title: The Game World — Data Structures
description: Start building your text adventure by defining the game world. Create a 'rooms' dictionary that maps room names to their descriptions (at least 3 rooms). Create an 'items' list with the names of items found in the world. Create a 'player' dictionary with 'name', 'health', and 'inventory' keys.
starter_code:
# Text Adventure — Step 1: The Game World
# Define the data structures for your game world.

# rooms: a dict mapping room name (str) to description (str)
# Include at least 3 rooms.
rooms = {
    # e.g. 'entrance': 'You stand at the entrance of a dark cave.',
}

# items: a list of item name strings found in the world
items = [
    # e.g. 'torch', 'sword', 'key'
]

# player: a dict with 'name' (str), 'health' (int), and 'inventory' (list)
player = {
    # Your player here
}

# Print the game world
print(f"World has {len(rooms)} rooms")
print(f"Items available: {items}")
print(f"Player: {player['name']} (HP: {player['health']})")
test_code:
assert isinstance(rooms, dict), 'rooms should be a dictionary'
assert len(rooms) >= 3, f'Need at least 3 rooms, got {len(rooms)}'
assert all(isinstance(k, str) and isinstance(v, str) for k, v in rooms.items()), 'Room keys and values should be strings'
assert isinstance(items, list), 'items should be a list'
assert len(items) >= 1, 'items should have at least 1 item'
assert isinstance(player, dict), 'player should be a dictionary'
assert 'name' in player, "player needs a 'name' key"
assert 'health' in player, "player needs a 'health' key"
assert 'inventory' in player, "player needs an 'inventory' key"
assert isinstance(player['name'], str), "player['name'] should be a string"
assert isinstance(player['health'], (int, float)), "player['health'] should be a number"
assert isinstance(player['inventory'], list), "player['inventory'] should be a list"
acceptance_criteria: rooms is a dict with at least 3 room name/description pairs
items is a list of item names
player is a dict with 'name', 'health', and 'inventory' keys
hints: A room entry looks like: 'cave': 'A dark cave with dripping water.'
The player dict might look like: {'name': 'Hero', 'health': 100, 'inventory': []}
You can start with an empty inventory list — items will be added later
